topic: A student receives a much lower grade than expected
raw_output:
[story 1]

The red ink swam across the paper, a damning indictment of my efforts. A C-minus. For the final project, after three weeks of relentless late nights fueled by lukewarm coffee and sheer panic, this was the reward? I stared at the grade as if it were a foreign object, utterly incomprehensible. My jaw tightened until a dull ache resonated behind my molars. I slowly pushed the paper away, the sound of the cheap printer whirring seeming deafening in the quiet dorm room. "Are you serious?" I muttered to the empty air, my voice tight and brittle. I picked up my pen, not to write anything, but just to grip it, feeling the tension coil tighter in my shoulders. This felt like a deliberate slight, a complete misreading of the assignment criteria.

[story 2]

I reread the feedback three times, my eyes skipping over the highlighted comments as if they were invisible. The numbers didn't lie; the score was undeniably low. A deep, slow sigh escaped my lungs, heavy with disappointment. I leaned back in my chair, the springs groaning in protest under my weight, and let my head fall against the cool plastic of the monitor. My hands clenched into fists on the desk, the knuckles turning white. "This is ridiculous," I whispered, the sound barely audible. I stood up abruptly, pacing the small space between my desk and the window, running a hand through my already messy hair. The frustration built into a hot, prickly sensation just beneath my skin.

[story 3]

When I saw the mark, a strange numbness washed over me, quickly followed by a sharp, hot surge of anger. It was a complete failure. I slammed my textbook shut, the sound echoing sharply in the lecture hall where I had spent the last semester trying to prove myself. I didn't bother to look at the professor; his presence felt irrelevant now. I started aggressively shuffling papers, tearing corners off drafts I’d already submitted. Every movement was jerky, fueled by a desperate need to lash out at the unfairness of the situation. I rubbed my temples hard, trying to physically rub the feeling out, but it just seemed to intensify, a tight knot refusing to loosen in my chest.
